gameFeature.title: Lurking Giants Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Survive terrifying night rounds in Lurking Giants, the Roblox analog-horror survival game where most players hide as Survivors while one towering Giant hunts them through Forest and City maps.

lurkingGiantsCodes.title: Lurking Giants Codes
lurkingGiantsCodes.subtitle: Redeem the latest working Lurking Giants code for free Coins. Copy the code exactly as displayed and claim it through the in-game Shop.
lurkingGiantsCodes.intro: Codes provide free Coins that can be saved for Giants and other Shop content. The current code was released with the Verity update and can be redeemed directly from the Codes section of the Shop.

items.description: No previously working Lurking Giants codes have been recorded as expired.

items.title: How to Redeem Lurking Giants Codes
steps: Launch Lurking Giants on Roblox and enter the lobby.
Select the Shop button on the left side of the screen.
Open the Codes section near the bottom of the Shop.
Press Enter or select the checkmark button to receive 200 Coins.

lurkingGiantsBeginnerGuide.title: Lurking Giants Beginner Guide
lurkingGiantsBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn how the lobby, role selection, survival rounds, Giant hunts, spectating, and Coin progression work before entering your first match.
lurkingGiantsBeginnerGuide.intro: Lurking Giants is an asymmetrical Roblox horror game in which most players become Survivors while another player controls a Giant. Survivors must remain alive through the night, while the Giant searches the map and attempts to eliminate everyone before the round ends.

steps.title: Prepare in the Lobby
steps.description: Use the lobby to check the Shop, redeem codes, inspect available Giants, and wait for the next match. Playing with friends makes it easier to share Giant sightings and coordinate hiding routes.

steps.title: Receive Your Role
steps.description: At the start of a match, players are assigned either the Survivor role or the Giant role. Survivor is the more common role, while Giant selection is controlled by the game's role-selection system.

steps.title: Survive as a Team
steps.description: Move between cover, watch open paths, and avoid standing in one large group. Keep at least one escape route nearby so the entire team is not trapped when the Giant reaches a hiding area.

steps.title: Use Map Cover
steps.description: Forest and City contain different terrain, sightlines, buildings, and hiding areas. Forest favors natural cover and route changes, while City provides structures and vertical paths that can break direct chases.

steps.title: React to Giant Sightings
steps.description: Do not run directly toward teammates when chased. Lead the Giant away from occupied hiding places, change direction around solid cover, and relocate after the Giant checks your area.

steps.title: Hunt as the Giant
steps.description: Patrol major paths instead of checking every small corner. Focus on cutting off exits, revisiting areas where Survivors were recently seen, and controlling routes between strong hiding locations.

steps.title: Spectate After Elimination
steps.description: Eliminated players can watch the remaining match. Use spectating to learn safe routes, common hiding places, Giant movement patterns, and mistakes that reveal Survivor positions.

steps.title: Earn and Spend Coins
steps.description: Complete rounds and use code rewards to build your Coin balance. Coins can be spent through the Shop on unlockable Giants and other progression content.

steps.title: First-Match Checklist
steps.description: Redeem the active code, learn one map route, stay near solid cover, avoid following large groups, watch the Giant's direction, and spectate the rest of the round after elimination.

lurkingGiantsTierList.title: Lurking Giants Giant Tier List
lurkingGiantsTierList.subtitle: Compare the current Giants by chase pressure, map control, accessibility, learning difficulty, and value for Coins.
lurkingGiantsTierList.intro: The best Giant depends on whether you want maximum pressure, a new update-focused playstyle, or a free character for learning the role. Guilt remains the strongest established progression purchase, Verity is the leading Verity-update option, and Locust is the most accessible starter.

items.name: Guilt
strengths: Strong chase pressure
Large and intimidating presence
Effective at controlling important routes
Established choice for dedicated Giant players
weaknesses: Less suitable for players still learning basic Giant routes
items.bestFor: Players who regularly receive the Giant role and want a long-term Coin purchase.

items.name: Verity
strengths: Designed around the latest Giant update
Strong pressure in active chase situations
Works well with the update's new mechanics and perks
Useful for players who already understand map routes
weaknesses: Requires more map knowledge than the starter Giant
New mechanics take time to learn
items.bestFor: Returning players who want to use the newest Giant and update mechanics.

items.name: Locust
strengths: Available without spending Coins
Simple choice for learning Giant movement
Good for practicing patrol routes and survivor tracking
No progression investment required
weaknesses: Lower overall pressure than premium Giants
Experienced Survivors are more familiar with its chase patterns
items.bestFor: New players learning how to patrol maps, find Survivors, and control escape routes.

lurkingGiantsGiantGuide.title: Lurking Giants Giant Guide
lurkingGiantsGiantGuide.subtitle: Meet Locust, Guilt, and Verity, then learn how to patrol maps, find Survivors, cut off escape paths, and finish chases.
lurkingGiantsGiantGuide.intro: Giants are the hunter side of each match. Their objective is to locate Survivors, pressure strong hiding locations, and eliminate players before the survival round ends. Each Giant offers a different balance of accessibility, chase pressure, and learning difficulty.

items.name: Locust
strengths: Free starter option
Easy to use for learning map routes
Effective when repeatedly checking busy paths
Suitable for practicing basic attacks and chase timing
weaknesses: Experienced Survivors recognize its common approach routes
Relies heavily on good positioning and route prediction
items.strategy: Patrol intersections and routes connecting major hiding areas. When a Survivor is found, move toward the next exit instead of following directly behind them.

items.name: Guilt
items.unlock: Purchase from the Shop for 7,000 Coins
strengths: Powerful presence during chases
Strong at forcing Survivors away from safe areas
Effective around narrow exits and important routes
High long-term value for frequent Giant players
weaknesses: Expensive compared with the default Giant
Poor route choices waste its strongest chase opportunities
items.strategy: Control the exits around buildings, caves, and other popular hiding zones. Force Survivors into open paths before committing to the final attack.

items.name: Verity
items.unlock: Available through the current Verity update roster and Shop
strengths: Built for the newest version of the game
Strong option for active and aggressive Giant players
Pairs with the update's expanded mechanics
Effective when the player already understands Survivor routes
weaknesses: Less forgiving when abilities or chase opportunities are mistimed
items.strategy: Use map knowledge to predict where Survivors will relocate after being discovered. Pressure the destination first and block the strongest escape route.

items.name: Giant Match Strategy
strengths: Patrol high-traffic routes instead of random corners
Check strong hiding areas from an angle that blocks exits
Track where Survivors are moving rather than where they were last seen
Return to recently cleared areas after Survivors begin relocating
Use spectating and Survivor rounds to study hiding habits
weaknesses: Long, direct chases can waste the remaining round time
Repeatedly checking empty areas gives Survivors time to reposition
items.strategy: Think one route ahead. The fastest elimination usually comes from intercepting a Survivor's destination rather than copying every turn they make.

lurkingGiantsControls.title: Lurking Giants PC and Mobile Controls
lurkingGiantsControls.subtitle: Learn the essential survivor and Giant inputs before entering a match. Controller and VR support are also part of the ongoing rework.
lurkingGiantsControls.intro: Most actions use standard Roblox movement controls, but sprinting uses E instead of Shift on PC. Giant players receive additional attack and Haunt controls, while Shop, Inventory, Configuration, and Spectate are opened through the on-screen interface.

items.notes: Move forward, left, backward, and right.
Look around and track the Giant while running or hiding.
The sprint key is E rather than the standard Roblox Shift key.
Used for basic traversal and reaching elevated routes.
Use the displayed Roblox interaction prompt when an object or action is available.
Performs the Giant's primary attack or attack sequence.
Activates the Giant's secondary Haunt ability.

items.input: Shop, Inventory, Configuration, and Spectate buttons
items.notes: Select the labeled interface button instead of using a fixed keyboard shortcut.
Drag the joystick in the desired direction.
Swipe to look around and check nearby routes.
The dedicated sprint button performs the same action as E on PC.
Giant-specific buttons appear when the player receives the Giant role.
The official Roblox description lists controller and VR support as upcoming features.

lurkingGiantsMaps.title: Lurking Giants Maps and Hiding Spots
lurkingGiantsMaps.subtitle: Use darkness, vertical routes, caves, structures, and solid cover to break the Giant's line of sight. Every hiding place should also have a planned exit.
lurkingGiantsMaps.intro: Forest and City environments reward different movement styles. Outdoor areas provide natural cover and dark terrain, while urban areas offer rooftops, stairways, corners, and elevated structures. Staying in one obvious location for the entire round makes it easier for the Giant to search that area.

items.title: Forest Map
items.summary: An outdoor environment with trees, bushes, caves, uneven ground, and wide open crossings.
details: Use tree lines and dense vegetation to interrupt the Giant's view.
Caves and dark recesses provide strong temporary concealment.
Avoid crossing exposed ground while the Giant is already facing your route.
Move to a second covered area after the Giant checks your current position.

items.title: City Map
items.summary: A vertical environment built around buildings, rooftops, stairs, corners, and elevated structures.
details: Use building corners and stair turns to break direct line of sight.
Rooftops and upper levels can create distance from a Giant below.
Choose elevated positions with a second stairway, drop route, or nearby platform.
Avoid rooftop dead ends that leave only one escape path.

items.title: Caves and Dark Recesses
items.summary: Dark enclosed areas can hide a survivor's silhouette and block long sightlines.
details: Stay away from the entrance so your avatar is not outlined against the opening.
Listen for the Giant before leaving the area.
Treat caves as temporary cover because the entrance can become a trap.
Exit after the Giant passes instead of waiting until it enters.

items.title: Rooftops and High Positions
items.summary: Elevated locations give survivors more time to see a Giant approaching.
details: Check the full route before climbing to the highest point.
Keep at least one alternative descent or connecting platform available.
Do not remain silhouetted on an exposed roof edge.
Leave early when the Giant begins climbing toward the same level.

items.title: Corners and Solid Obstacles
items.summary: Walls, buildings, rocks, and other large objects are more dependable than open darkness.
details: Keep the obstacle between your avatar and the Giant.
Move around the opposite side when the Giant changes direction.
Avoid small objects that do not fully hide your avatar.
Use several nearby obstacles as a rotation route rather than one permanent hiding point.

items.title: Safe Rotation Rule
items.summary: A good hiding spot becomes dangerous after the Giant discovers or repeatedly checks it.
details: Watch the Giant's direction before leaving cover.
Sprint only when movement is necessary and a destination has already been chosen.
Do not stack the entire survivor team in one cave or rooftop.
Rotate to a new covered position after the Giant moves past your lane.

lurkingGiantsCoins.title: Lurking Giants Coins, Giants, and Unlocks
lurkingGiantsCoins.subtitle: Spend Coins on permanent progression after learning the free Giant. Roblox passes remain optional and are not required to enter normal matches.
lurkingGiantsCoins.intro: Coins are the main earnable currency used for shop progression. New players can begin with Locust, claim the available free code reward, and save toward Guilt before considering optional Robux passes.

items.name: Locust
items.priority: Use Locust to learn Giant movement, attacking, Haunt timing, and map routes before buying another Giant.

items.name: Guilt
items.priority: The main long-term Coin purchase for players who regularly enjoy the Giant role.

items.name: SORRYFORDELAY
items.howToGet: Open Shop, find the code field, enter the code, and redeem it
items.priority: Grants 200 Coins and should be claimed before beginning the longer Guilt savings goal.

items.name: Match Coins
items.howToGet: Complete rounds and perform the objectives of the assigned survivor or Giant role
items.priority: Continue playing normal matches after claiming available code rewards.

items.name: 2x Giant Chance
items.howToGet: Purchase from the Roblox Store section
items.priority: Only useful for players who specifically want the Giant role more often; it does not replace a Giant unlock.

items.name: 2x Voting Power
items.howToGet: Purchase from the Roblox Store section
items.priority: A convenience purchase for influencing map or match voting rather than direct progression.

items.name: Powerful Flashlight
items.howToGet: Purchase from the Roblox Store section
items.priority: Provides a visibility-focused option for players who frequently choose the survivor role.

items.name: VIP
items.howToGet: Purchase from the Roblox Store section
items.priority: Check the current Store description before purchasing because VIP benefits can change during the rework.

lurkingGiantsUpdates.title: Lurking Giants Updates and Massive Rework
lurkingGiantsUpdates.subtitle: Follow released Verity content, reworked systems, balance changes, and the remaining device and gameplay features announced by ULTRA works.
lurkingGiantsUpdates.intro: Lurking Giants is being rebuilt through a large multi-feature rework. The current Verity update introduced a new Giant-focused release alongside player perks, codes, map changes, balancing, broader device support, and bug fixes, while additional movement and platform features remain on the roadmap.

items.title: Massive Rework and New Mechanics
changes: The official Roblox description confirms a major rework.
Core systems, maps, balance, and device support are being updated.
The developer warns that new minor bugs may appear while systems are rebuilt.

items.title: Verity Giant Update
changes: Verity was added as the headline Giant for the update.
The Roblox experience title changed to Lurking Giants [VERITY].
New survivor classes and player perks became part of the updated gameplay.

items.title: Codes and Coin Rewards
changes: A code redemption system was added to the Shop.
SORRYFORDELAY rewards 200 Coins.
Coins can be used toward shop progression and Giant unlocks.

items.title: Balance and Giant Selection Changes
changes: Giant balancing was included in the official event notes.
Giant selection chances were improved.
Existing Giant and survivor interactions received rework-era adjustments.

items.title: Reworked Maps and Device Improvements
changes: Existing maps received rework changes.
New device support was included in the update.
Bug fixes accompanied the new gameplay systems.

items.title: Advanced Movement and Expanded Combat
changes: Advanced movement is listed as a planned rework feature.
New ways for survivors to attack or resist the Giant are planned.
Additional items and expanded Giant abilities are part of the broader roadmap.

items.title: Additional Giants, Maps, Classes, and Perks
changes: More Giants are planned beyond the current roster.
New maps are included in the official update roadmap.
Player classes and perks are expected to continue expanding.

items.title: Controller and VR Support
changes: The official Roblox description lists controller support as coming soon.
VR support is also listed as an upcoming platform feature.
Final bindings and supported device details will be shown when these features are released.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Lurking Giants

questions.question: What are Lurking Giants codes and how do I redeem them?
questions.answer: Lurking Giants codes are free rewards claimed through the in-game Shop. Open the Shop, find the Codes section, enter the active code exactly as shown, and confirm to receive Coins. The current code SORRYFORDELAY grants 200 Coins.

questions.question: How do I survive a full round in Lurking Giants?
questions.answer: In Lurking Giants, most players become Survivors and must stay alive through the night while one Giant hunts the map. Move between solid cover, keep an escape route open, avoid stacking in one spot, and rotate to a new hiding area after the Giant checks your position.

questions.question: Which Giant should I unlock first in Lurking Giants?
questions.answer: New Lurking Giants players should learn the role with Locust, the free starter Giant. Once you regularly receive the Giant role, save 7,000 Coins for Guilt, the strongest long-term chase pressure purchase, or pick Verity from the current update roster.

questions.question: What are player perks in Lurking Giants?
questions.answer: Player perks are survivor bonuses introduced in the Lurking Giants Verity update. They expand progression alongside new survivor classes, and additional perks are expected to keep arriving as the rework continues.

questions.question: Can I play Lurking Giants on mobile and with a controller?
questions.answer: Yes. Lurking Giants runs on PC and mobile through Roblox today, using a virtual joystick, swipe camera, and on-screen sprint and ability buttons. Controller and VR support are listed in the official description as upcoming features in the rework.

questions.question: Is Lurking Giants free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Lurking Giants is free to play on Roblox. Coins are earned by completing rounds and redeeming codes, while optional Robux passes like 2x Giant Chance or the Powerful Flashlight are convenience items and are never required to enter a match.
